plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to boost income and broad market stability.
Buy XLK for quality tech exposure, adhering to single name limits.
This plan significantly de-risks the portfolio by reducing leverage and concentration.
A substantial cash buffer would be held after these moves.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces overall portfolio risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also more modest potential gains.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL entirely to remove a high-risk leveraged holding.
Trim SPAL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure within limits.
Slightly reduce QQQI to balance the income component with growth.
Buy XLK and add to SMH to increase exposure to quality tech and semiconductors.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% per month by blending broad index with quality tech and limited leverage. It aims for risk-first growth, reducing concentration and high-beta exposure. Expect moderate swings with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Increase SOXL and SPAL exposure, leveraging strong semiconductor and space trends.
Trim QQQI to redeploy capital into higher-growth opportunities.
Add significantly to SMH and buy META and XLK, focusing on top-scoring setups.
This plan leans into leverage and concentration for aggressive growth.
Strict stop discipline is crucial given the amplified risk and daily decay of leveraged ETFs.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth approach targets ~4% per month by stretching into leveraged ETFs and high-scoring setups. It significantly raises both potential upside and drawdown. Be aware of amplified losses, daily decay of leveraged ETFs, and the need for strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs, prioritizing low drawdown.
Trim CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Significantly add to QQQI to maximize monthly distribution potential.
This plan prioritizes steady income and capital preservation over growth.
A substantial cash buffer would be maintained for stability.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1% per month by rotating into income-generating ETFs and broad index. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, cutting leverage and high-beta names. Remember, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
